data.title: As a developer using /dso:brainstorm, a top-level anti-redundancy directive prevents duplicate questions
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add an explicit top-level anti-redundancy directive to `plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md`. Before forming any question, the agent must check (a) prior conversation turns semantically — recognising paraphrase and negative signals like "I told you", "I answered that", or semantically-equivalent rewordings of prior answers — and (b) the codebase via Grep / Read / Glob. Duplicate questions are prohibited.

**Why**: The UX probe set (S2) risks duplicating content the user already supplied during the Tell Me More loop; without a top-level directive, probes feel mechanical and erode user trust. The directive must be top-level (not scoped to Phase 1 Step 2.5) so it applies across every phase that may form questions.

**Scope**:
- IN:
  - Top-level anti-redundancy directive placed high in `plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md` so it applies across all phases.
  - Explicit semantic-check guidance: paraphrase detection and negative signals ("I told you", "I answered that", etc.).
  - Explicit codebase-check guidance: use Grep/Read/Glob before forming any question whose answer may live in the repo.
  - Regression test that seeds a user answer covering one of the three UX probe topics (from S2) and asserts the corresponding probe is suppressed in the recorded question log.
- OUT:
  - The UX probe set itself (S2).
  - Phase 1 Gate loop-back state machine (S3).
  - Non-interactive mode (S5).
  - E2E dogfooding test (S6) — note: S6 seed must be crafted to NOT pre-answer any probe so SC8 (all 3 probes asked) still passes when this directive is active.
  - Programmatic enforcement of the directive — compliance is agent-level, not hook-enforced, in this epic.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md` carries an explicit top-level anti-redundancy directive that requires the agent to (a) check prior conversation turns semantically (recognising paraphrase and negative signals like "I told you" / "I answered that") and (b) check the codebase via Grep/Read/Glob before forming any question. Duplicate questions are prohibited.
  ← Satisfies: "Brainstorm SKILL.md carries an explicit anti-redundancy directive: before forming any question, the agent must check (a) prior conversation turns semantically (recognising paraphrase and negative signals like 'I told you' / 'I answered that'), and (b) the codebase via Grep/Read/Glob. Duplicate questions prohibited."
- When this story is complete, a regression test seeds a user answer covering one of the three UX probe topics (S2) and asserts the corresponding probe is suppressed in the recorded question log.
  ← Satisfies: "Verified by: regression test that seeds a user answer covering one of the three UX probe topics and asserts the corresponding probe is suppressed in the recorded question log"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Directive must be top-level (placed before Phase 1 in SKILL.md, or in a clearly-scoped "Directives" block) so it applies across every brainstorm phase, not just Step 2.5.
- [Testing] Anti-redundancy suppresses duplicate probes; S6's E2E dogfooding test (SC8) asserts all 3 probes are asked. The S6 test seed must be crafted to NOT pre-answer any of the 3 probe topics (interaction criticality, non-happy-path state coverage, flow entry/exit) — otherwise this directive will suppress a probe and SC8 will fail. Cross-reference S6's consideration.
- [Interaction with S3] When this directive suppresses every candidate question during S3's loop-back, S3 records UNRESOLVABLE_INTENT_GAP and terminates — the escape hatch is in S3, not here.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
